message.value: "{0}" modifier out of order with the JLS suggestions. The correct order: public protected private abstract default static final transient volatile synchronized native strictfp. See https://crbug.com/1003711 for details.

property.name: tokens
property.value: INTERFACE_DEF, CLASS_DEF

message.value: Public classes and interfaces require JavaDoc comments.

Static final field names must either be all caps (e.g. int HEIGHT_PX) for 'true' constants, or start with s (e.g. AtomicInteger sNextId or Runnable sSuspendTask) for fields with mutable state or that don't 'feel' like constants.
Non-public, non-static field names start with m.
Static field names start with s.
Method names should start with a lower case letter (e.g. getWidth())
Local variables should be camel-cased (e.g. int minWidth = 4).
Local variables should be camel-cased (e.g. int minWidth = 4).

property.name: message
property.value: Avoid StringBuffer; use StringBuilder instead, which is faster (it's not thread-safe, but this is almost never needed)

property.name: message
property.value: Avoid android.app.AlertDialog; if possible, use android.support.v7.app.AlertDialog instead, which has a Material look on all devices. (Some parts of the codebase canâ€™t depend on the support library, in which case android.app.AlertDialog is the only option)

property.name: message
property.value: Use SharedPreferencesManager instead to access app-wide SharedPreferences from //src/chrome.

property.name: message
property.value: Use ContextUtils.getAppSharedPreferences() instead to access app-wide SharedPreferences.

property.name: message
property.value: ConcurrentHashMap has a bug on some Android versions. Use Collections.synchronizedMap() instead (crbug.com/905903).

property.name: format
property.value: try .*StrictModeContext (?!ignored).*=

property.name: message
property.value: Please name the StrictModeContext variable "ignored" (crbug.com/767058#c4).

property.name: message
property.value: Throw an exception instead of calling System#exit (crbug.com/1000651).

property.name: format
property.value: extends\ Button|extends\ AppCompatButton

property.name: message
property.value: Use org.chromium.ui.widget.ButtonCompat instead of Button and AppCompatButton if possible; ButtonCompat is a Material-styled button with a customizable background color. On L devices, this is a true Material button. On earlier devices, the button is similar but lacks ripples and a shadow. (crbug.com/775198)

property.name: message
property.value: If possible, please use #setTextAppearance to set one of the pre-defined text appearance styles in src/ui/android/java/res/values-v17/styles.xml, instead of using #setTextColor or #setTextSize. This will ensure that your programmatically set text styles stay in-sync with text styles used in the rest of Chrome. (crbug.com/775198)

property.name: message
property.value: Launching an ActivityScenario manually is error prone for any test run in a batch. Failing to #close will cause the Activity to leak into the next test. Consider replacing with ActivityScenarioRule. (https://crbug.com/1338271)

property.name: format
property.value: import\ com.google.common.base.Optional;

property.name: message
property.value: Use java.util.Optional instead of com.google.common.base.Optional unless required to interact with external libraries.

property.name: message
property.value: Subclassing collections is uncommon. If you are not doing so, please ignore. If you are subclassing in order to call add()/put(), use List.of() / Set.of() / Map.of() instead, or use multiple statements.

property.name: message
property.value: Use org.chromium.ui.accessibility.AccessibilityState instead of AccessibilityManager. For more information, contact //ui/accessibility/OWNERS.

property.name: message
property.value: Fullscreen AlertDialogs must use FullscreenAlertDialog and fullscreen Dialogs must use ChromeDialog. Not doing this will break automotive devices. (This check is triggered by the presence of ThemeOverlay_BrowserUI_Fullscreen and does not know if you are already using the correct class)

property.name: message
property.value:
Please use inclusive language where possible.
Instead of 'dummy', use a term such as placeholder or empty (e.g. PlaceholderImpl, not DummyImpl). Instead of 'sanity', use a term such as 'confidence' (e.g. confidence check, not sanity check). Instead of 'blind', use a term such as unaware (e.g. being unaware of, not being blind to. unconfirmed change, not blind change).
For more info see: https://developers.google.com/style/word-list

property.name: format
property.value: PreferenceFragment.*\ extends\ (PreferenceFragment|ChromeBaseSettingsFragment)

property.name: message
property.value: Top level settings fragment should be named with suffix 'SettingsFragment' or 'Settings'. See //components/browser_ui/settings/README.

property.name: message
property.value: Parameter comments should use the ErrorProne-aware syntax: /* paramName= */.

property.name: message
property.value: Each method reference creates a new instance. The specific object given to the addObserver-like-method needs to be remembered and given to the removeObserver-like-method.

property.name: message
property.value: Prefer to use MockitoJUnit.Rule for consistency unless timing of when exactly mocks are initialized is required.

property.name: message
property.value: setDecorFitsSystemWindows will change the window flag at activity level, and requires coordination. Use org.chromium.browser_ui.edge_to_edge.EdgeToEdgeStateProvider if possible. (crbug.com/41492646)

property.name: message
property.value: There is no need to add @VisibleForTesting to test-only methods (those with "ForTesting" suffix).
